source_pr.title: restore: remove restore meta sql in create table
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Speed up create tables via omit a DDL job.

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed: Remove all DDLs of rebasing autos.

How it Works: When creating table with `CreateTableWithInfo`, TiDB has rebased the auto ids via the table info:

https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/a9cba7c8e8b6da512609863b56ee7265c06c50dc/ddl/ddl_api.go#L1924-L1937

It's no meaning for rebasing the auto ids again.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Integration test (at `br_views_and_sequences`)

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
Merged the rebase auto id operation into create table.
```

test.name: restore: remove restore meta sql in create table

symptoms: last_id=$(run_sql "select n from $DB.auto_inc order by n desc limit 1" | tail -n1) (br/tests/br_views_and_sequences/run.sh)
failure_signature: last_id=$(run_sql "select n from $DB.auto_inc order by n desc limit 1" | tail -n1) (br/tests/br_views_and_sequences/run.sh)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: last_id=$(run_sql "select n from $DB.auto_inc order by n desc limit 1" | tail -n1) (br/tests/br_views_and_sequences/run.sh).
fix_pattern: last_id=$(run_sql "select n from $DB.auto_inc order by n desc limit 1" | tail -n1) (br/tests/br_views_and_sequences/run.sh)

evidence: evidence: last_id=$(run_sql "select n from $DB.auto_inc order by n desc limit 1" | tail -n1) (br/tests/br_views_and_sequences/run.sh)
